definition: GDSN is a registry that enables trading partners to globally share trusted product data. It supports accurate, real-time data sharing and trade item updates among subscribed trading partners.

Term used to describe a type of setup that contains a full unit load of product. Variations of Displays are Mod, Half Mod and Quarter Mod (all three are built using trays), Rainbow Mod (built using full cases of product) and Shippers (built using printed corrugate).

These are promotional variants of Base, Every Day Displays (Mods, Half Mods, Shippers, etc). They share UPC’s with the Parent or Base item. They do not have pricing loaded, but instead, have a pricing reference which is the Parent or Base item. Customers continue to order the Parent or Base item, and substitution tables are entered into SAP to move it to the Promo Code. This setup type has its own form, separate from the New Item Form Workflow. See also, Promo Code.

This field is located in the Classification View in the Material Master, and is used by EDI to determine how an item is handled on orders. There are three choices, RETAIL, CLUB PACK and SINGLE SERVE. CLUB PACK and SINGLE SERVE have logic built around them, and RETAIL has none. See EDI Logic Club Pack and EDI Logic Single Serve for more details.

This field is located in the Classification View in the Material Master, and is used by the AFH (Away From Home, formerly Specialty Channels) team. There are a variety of choices, but Retail items will always be set up as GROCERY, Club items will always use CLUB PACK, Single Serve items will always use SINGLE SERVE, Bulk items will always use BULK and AFH owned items will use all others.

SAP stands for "Systems, Applications, and Products in Data Processing." Originally, it was known in German as "Systemanalyse und Programmentwicklung," which translates to "System Analysis and Program Development." SAP is a leading producer of enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, helping businesses manage their operations more efficiently.

Supply Chain Request. Projects that are initiated by Supply Chain or the business unit, for the purpose to improve supply chain capabilities, efficiencies, and cost savings.  These projects require wide supply chain integration. Must be integrated with all the PDR and PWR projects to avoid execution conflicts.
Term used to describe an item that can be found (for example) at the checkout, where the consumer can purchase just one “single serve” vs an entire package or carton.
Often used to describe the same thing.
Service Level Agreement. Each role in the workflow has a designated number of days to perform their task, which is called an SLA.  Most tasks have 2 days. Some tasks have as many as 7 days.
Away From Home sales district or business unit. Businesses where Kellanova sells food that doesn't sell for at-home consumption. (EX: Restaurants, Convenience stores, Vending machines).
Special K

Single-serve items are products packaged in individual units designed for one-time use or consumption by a single end user. These items support portion control, convenience, and are commonly used in retail, foodservice, and hospitality settings. In terms of the Single Serve Product Type these items have special considerations for how they need to be setup in SAP for TPM planning and EDI logic.
Term used to describe an item that is a basic setup type. It consists of a case and a package, or a case, package and a pouch level.

term: SUL/TUL, SUL TUL, SUL, or TUL
definition: Shipping Unit Load / Trailer Unit Load. SUL is the Shipping Unit Load (maximum inventory that can be fit into the trailer if NOT tiered), TUL is the Trailer Unit Load (maximum inventory that can be fit into the trailer if tiered).

Term used to describe a type of setup for product that is not sold to customers, but used to transfer food within and between plants. There are two kinds of WIP’s, one is finished product in cases (WIP Case), and the other is large totes of food, cereal flakes for example (WIP Tote). Both are set up with the Material Type of HALB.
Wholesome Snacks

The EDI_MATERIAL_XREF table is a customer exception based table that is customer (Trading Partner in EDI verbiage) and material specific.  It is a table where the EDI Team puts in the UPC or GTIN that is ordered by the customer and points it directly to a Kellanova material.  There is no logic behind it.  If the Trading Partnership and UPC or GTIN is present in the table, SAP will post to the material that is populated in the table.  It is primarily used for Dollar customers that order with package UPC code and that need to receive the Reduced Case Pack (RCP) material. This is because RCP materials and Base Materials often share the same package UPC. When a customer orders using a package UPC that is shared among many items, SAP can only post to materials marked as Base Materials, and RCP materials are not marked as Base Materials.  This table overrides that material determination logic to ensure that the Dollar customers can receive the correct RCP materials.
Material Status in SAP which means New. At this point the item is visible in SAP and plannable, but not yet in production.
Material Status in SAP which means Active. At this point the item has been costed by Finance, and is nearing or in production.
Selling price / Red K price condition record
Material Master Update table in SAP. This is a transaction used by Data Operations to extend items to different locations based on item setup type.
